prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, a widely used approach for simulating stock prices. This model assumes that daily logarithmic returns are normally distributed with a constant mean (drift) and standard deviation (volatility). For PEP, historical data over the past year is used to estimate these parameters. We assume an average annualized drift of approximately 7% and an annualized volatility of 20%, which are typical for a stable, large-cap stock. The current price of $168.24 is projected 21 days into the future. The point estimate represents the median of the predicted log-normal price distribution, indicating the most likely outcome. The confidence intervals are derived from the quantiles of this distribution, showing the range within which the price is expected to fall with the specified probability (50%, 80%, or 90%). The model inherently assumes that past price behavior will continue into the future and does not account for unforeseen market-moving events, fundamental company changes, or shifts in macroeconomic conditions.
